# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a ledger-style record of all routing-related files examined during the Hono routing system analysis. Each file is documented with its purpose, notable names, and a simple note.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main entry point for the Hono framework; instantiates SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as default.
- Names noticed: `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `HonoOptions`, `SmartRouter`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `BlankEnv`, `BlankSchema`, `Env`, `Schema`.
- Simple note: The constructor defaults to SmartRouter when no router is explicitly passed in options.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base class providing core Hono functionality including error handling, middleware composition, and router integration.
- Names noticed: `ErrorHandler`, `errorHandler`, `onError`, `compose`, `Context`, `Route`, `BaseRouter`, `app`, `router`, `middleware`, `handleRequest`, `fetchHandler`.
- Simple note: Handles routing execution, error catching, and middleware chains through composition.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports PatternRouter class for pattern-based routing.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`.
- Simple note: Single-line export statement directing to router implementation.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements pattern-based routing using regular expressions for matching paths.
- Names noticed: `Route`, `emptyParams`, `PatternRouter`, `Router`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `routeMethod`, `match`, `handler`, `parts`, `RegExp`.
- Simple note: Converts path patterns into regex and stores as route array for matching.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports LinearRouter class for linear traversal routing.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`.
- Simple note: Single-line export statement directing to router implementation.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements linear router using array traversal with support for wildcards and labeled parameters.
- Names noticed: `RegExpMatchArrayWithIndices`, `emptyParams`, `LinearRouter`, `Router`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `splitPathRe`, `splitByStarRe`, `ROUTES_LOOP`, `routeMethod`, `routePath`, `handler`, `hasStar`, `hasLabel`, `pos`, `index`, `params`.
- Simple note: Processes routes sequentially; uses loops with early continue for mismatches.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter classes.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`.
- Simple note: Exports primary RegExpRouter and performance-optimized Prepared variant.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Main RegExp-based router implementation with middleware support and regex pattern matching.
- Names noticed: `ParamIndexMap`, `Router`, `MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `match`, `emptyParam`, `PATH_ERROR`, `wildcardRegExpCache`, `buildWildcardRegExp`, `buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes`, `findMiddleware`, `HandlerWithMetadata`, `nullMatcher`, `RegExpRouter`, `buildAllMatchers`, `buildMatcher`.
- Simple note: Builds matcher at runtime; caches wildcards and delegates to match helper.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines matcher type and match function for regex-based routing matching.
- Names noticed: `ParamIndexMap`, `Result`, `Router`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `emptyParam`, `match`.
- Simple note: Constructs match function that caches and uses regex/static map for lookup.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Provides pre-built RegExpRouter for scenarios requiring upfront path knowledge.
- Names noticed: `ParamIndexMap`, `Result`, `Router`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `HandlerData`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `StaticMap`, `RelocateMap`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`.
- Simple note: Pre-creates matcher during initialization for faster subsequent adds and matches.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Trie node class for building regex patterns from path tokens.
- Names noticed: `PATH_ERROR`, `ParamAssocArray`, `Context`, `regExpMetaChars`, `compareKey`, `ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `LABEL_REG_EXP_EXP_STR`, `TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `Node`, `insert`, `buildRegExpStr`, `varIndex`, `children`, `tokens`, `regexpStr`.
- Simple note: Recursively builds regex string from path tokens during insertion.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Trie data structure that organizes path tokens for regex generation.
- Names noticed: `Context`, `ParamAssocArray`, `Node`, `ReplacementMap`, `Trie`, `pathErrorCheckOnly`, `paramAssoc`, `groups`, `tokens`, `regexp`, `captureIndex`, `indexReplacementMap`, `paramReplacementMap`.
- Simple note: Uses groups to replace parameter patterns and delegates to Node for regex building.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports SmartRouter class for multi-router orchestration.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`.
- Simple note: Single-line export statement directing to router implementation.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Orchestrates multiple routers; tries each sequentially and delegates to best match.
- Names noticed: `Result`, `Router`, `MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `SmartRouter`, `activeRouter`, `routers`, `routes`, `match`, `add`.
- Simple note: Iterates through router array; commits to first successful match.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file that re-exports TrieRouter class.
- Names noticed: `TrieRouter`.
- Simple note: Single-line export statement directing to router implementation.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements trie-based routing for efficient path matching.
- Names noticed: `Result`, `Router`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `Node`, `TrieRouter`, `node`, `insert`, `search`, `results`.
- Simple note: Delegates to Node class for path insertion and traversal during search.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Advanced trie node with pattern matching for complex routing scenarios.
- Names noticed: `Params`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `Pattern`, `getPattern`, `splitPath`, `splitRoutingPath`, `HandlerSet`, `HandlerParamsSet`, `emptyParams`, `hasChildren`, `Node`, `children`, `patterns`, `order`, `params`, `method`, `handler`, `insert`, `search`, `pushHandlerSets`.
- Simple note: Maintains method handlers and pattern lists; performs pattern matching during search.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test suite that runs common routing tests across all router implementations.
- Names noticed: `RunnerTestSuite`, `ParamIndexMap`, `Params`, `Router`, `runTest`, `skip`, `newRouter`, `task`, `suite`, `skipTask`, `name`, `isSkip`, `beforeEach`, `afterEach`, `match`, `router`.
- Simple note: Provides test scaffolding with skip capability for known limitations.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for PatternRouter covering duplicate params and trailing slash handling.
- Names noticed: `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `PatternRouter`, `duplicate param name`, `Duplicate param name`, `self`, `Trailing slash`, `Trailing slash > GET /book/`.
- Simple note: Runs common tests with specific skips for PatternRouter limitations.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for LinearRouter including multi-match and trailing slash scenarios.
- Names noticed: `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `LinearRouter`, `Multi match`, 'params' per a handler, `Trailing slash`, `Skip part`, `GET /products/list`.
- Simple note: Runs common tests with skips for unsupported path patterns.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for RegExpRouter covering ambiguous paths and unsupported patterns.
- Names noticed: `ParamStash`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `runTest`, `RegExpRouter`, `Return value type`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `Ambiguous`, `Multiple handlers`, `parent`, `child`, `static and dynamic`, `Capture Group`, `Complex capturing group`.
- Simple note: Comprehensive coverage including error cases for ambiguous patterns.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests verifying SmartRouter delegates correctly to inner routers.
- Names noticed: `runTest`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `SmartRouter`, `newRouter`.
- Simple note: Uses default SmartRouter composed with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Basic unit tests for TrieRouter via common test suite.
- Names noticed: `runTest`, `TrieRouter`.
- Simple note: Runs all common tests against TrieRouter.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Comprehensive unit tests for TrieRouter node including pattern matching.
- Names noticed: `Params`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `Pattern`, `Node`, `pushHandlerSets`, `insert`, `search`, `Blog`, `failed`, `HandlerSet`, `handlerSets`, `sort`, `score`, `matcher`, `RegExp`.
- Simple note: Contains detailed pattern matching tests with complex regex scenarios.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router

- Exact file paths:
  - `src/router/pattern-router/index.ts`
  - `src/router/pattern-router/router.ts`
  - `src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts`
- Key characteristics: Regular expression-based pattern matching, converts path segments to regex, supports wildcard at end, throws UnsupportedPathError for duplicate params.

### linear-router

- Exact file paths:
  - `src/router/linear-router/index.ts`
  - `src/router/linear-router/router.ts`
  - `src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts`
- Key characteristics: Array-based linear traversal, supports trailing wildcard with label support, processes routes sequentially with early exit on mismatch.

### reg-exp-router

- Exact file paths:
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts`
  - `src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts`
- Key characteristics: Two-phase build-time preparation, uses trie to generate complex regex, caches wildcards, supports middleware, has Prepared variant for pre-built performance.

### smart-router

- Exact file paths:
  - `src/router/smart-router/index.ts`
  - `src/router/smart-router/router.ts`
  - `src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts`
- Key characteristics: Router aggregator that tries multiple routers sequentially, commits to first match, used by default in Hono constructor.

### trie-router

- Exact file paths:
  - `src/router/trie-router/index.ts`
  - `src/router/trie-router/router.ts`
  - `src/router/trie-router/node.ts`
  - `src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts`
  - `src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts`
- Key characteristics: Tree-based structure with node-based matching, supports labeled parameters with regex patterns, delegates search and insert to Node class.

---

## Verification Status

- Files read: 24
- Directory created: `bench-artifacts/`
- Output file created: `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`
- File size check: Document generated with all 24 file sections and cross-file checklist
- Source code modified: No modifications made to any source files
- Only file created in this task: `bench-artifacts/routing-read-write-ledger.md`

---

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a comprehensive read ledger of all routing-related source files in the Hono framework. It documents the purpose, key names, and a simple note for each file as part of a read/write endurance benchmark task.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and sets up routing with a default SmartRouter combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `SmartRouter`, `RegExpRouter`, `TrieRouter`, `HonoOptions`, `BlankEnv`, `BlankSchema`, `Env`, `Schema`, `methods` array (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS, PATCH, ALL).
- Simple note: Constructor initializes router to SmartRouter by default with `[new RegExpRouter(), new TrieRouter()]` fallback configuration.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module implementing the core Hono application class with routing, middleware composition, error handling, and fetch/request entry points.
- Names noticed: `Hono`, `HonoBase`, `Router`, `Context`, `Context` (execution), `compose`, `getPath`, `getPathNoStrict`, `mergePath`, `METHODS`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL_LOWERCASE`, `ErrorHandler`, `NotFoundHandler`, `RouterRoute`, `_basePath`, `#path`, `routes`, `notFoundHandler`, `errorHandler`, `#dispatch`, `#addRoute`.
- Simple note: Implements handler registration via `get()`, `post()`, `use()`, `route()`, `basePath()`, `mount()`, and request dispatch through `#dispatch()` which calls `router.match()`.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module entry point for PatternRouter, re-exporting the router implementation from router.ts.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `router`.
- Simple note: Single file that delegates all exports to the implementation module.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using compiled regular expressions for route matching with wildcard and parameter support.
- Names noticed: `PatternRouter`, `Route` (type), `Params`, `Result`, `Router`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `emptyParams`, `#routes` (array of `[RegExp, string, handler]`), `endsWithWildcard`, `match` (regex pattern), `replace(/[.\\+*[^\]$()]/g, '\\$&')`.
- Simple note: Converts paths with `:param` syntax to RegExp patterns using `new RegExp()` and matches routes linearly through the `#routes` array.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module entry point for LinearRouter, re-exporting the router implementation from router.ts.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `router`.
- Simple note: Single file that delegates all exports to the implementation module.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter with linear search through routes, supporting star wildcards (`*`) and parameterized routes with `:param` syntax.
- Names noticed: `LinearRouter`, `Params`, `Result`, `Router`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `RouterMatchArrayWithIndices`, `splitPathRe`, `splitByStarRe`, `emptyParams`, `#routes` (array of `[string, string, handler]`), `ROUTES_LOOP`, `indexOf(part, pos)`, `hasStar`, `hasLabel`.
- Simple note: Sequential route matching with special handling for `*` wildcards and `:param` labels via string splitting and index-based position tracking.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module entry point for RegExpRouter, re-exporting the main router and PreparedRegExpRouter with build/serialize helpers.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`, `router`, `router` (exports).
- Simple note: Exposes both standard RegExpRouter (lazy matcher building) and PreparedRegExpRouter (pre-built matchers for edge workers).

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter that builds compiled regular expressions from routes for high-performance matching with middleware and wildcard support.
- Names noticed: `RegExpRouter`, `ParamIndexMap`, `Router`, `MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT`, `METHOD_NAME_ALL`, `UnsupportedPathError`, `checkOptionalParameter`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `match`, `emptyParam`, `PATH_ERROR`, `wildcardRegExpCache`, `buildWildcardRegExp`, `buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes`, `Trie`, `findMiddleware`, `HandlerWithMetadata`, `#middleware`, `#routes`, `#buildMatcher`, `buildAllMatchers`, `clearWildcardRegExpCache`.
- Simple note: Routes are stored in `#middleware` and `#routes` dictionaries, then compiled into a single RegExp with handler maps for fast matching.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines types for RegExpRouter matching and provides the core `match` function that executes pattern matching against compiled matchers.
- Names noticed: `ParamIndexMap`, `Result`, `Router`, `HandlerData`, `StaticMap`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `match`, `emptyParam`, `buildAllMatchers`.
- Simple note: `match()` function retrieves pre-built matcher, checks `staticMap` first, then executes RegExp and maps indices via `matcher[1][index]`.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter with pre-compiled matchers for performance-critical environments like Cloudflare Workers edge functions.
- Names noticed: `PreparedRegExpRouter`, `Router`, `HandlerData`, `Matcher`, `MatcherMap`, `StaticMap`, `match`, `emptyParam`, `RelocateMap`, `#matchers`, `#relocateMap`, `#addWildcard`, `#addPath`, `buildInitParams`, `serializeInitParams`, `buildAndExportAllMatchers`.
- Simple note: Takes pre-constructed `matchers` and `relocateMap` in constructor for zero-overhead matching in edge environments.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Defines Node class for trie-based pattern compilation used by RegExpRouter to build regular expressions from route paths.
- Names noticed: `Node`, `ParamAssocArray`, `Context`, `PATH_ERROR`, `LABEL_REG_EXP_STR`, `ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR`, `regExpMetaChars`, `compareKey`, `insert`, `#index`, `#varIndex`, `#children`, `buildRegExpStr`.
- Simple note: Trie structure with `#children` map storing child nodes, `insert()` builds tokenized path into pattern, `buildRegExpStr()` assembles final regex.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class that processes path patterns, handles named groups with `{regex}` syntax, and builds the RegExp compilation graph.
- Names noticed: `Trie`, `Context`, `ParamAssocArray`, `ReplacementMap`, `Node`, `insert`, `buildRegExp`, `#context`, `#root`, `groups`, `tokens`, `paramReplacementMap`, `indexReplacementMap`.
- Simple note: Tokenizes paths, extracts named groups via `@\d` markers, delegates to Node for insertion, `buildRegExp()` returns `[RegExp, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap]`.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module entry point for SmartRouter, re-exporting the router implementation from router.ts.
- Names noticed: `SmartRouter`, `router`.
- Simple note: Single file that delegates all exports to the implementation module.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter as a router-orchestrator that tries multiple routers sequentially and adopts the first one that successfully matches routes.

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and configures the default SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, HonoOptions.
- Simple note: Default router configuration uses SmartRouter which tries RegExpRouter first, then TrieRouter as a fallback.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Core Hono framework class providing HTTP method handlers, middleware support, routing, error handling, and request dispatching.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, getPath, mergePath, errorHandler, notFoundHandler, COMPOSED_HANDLER, #dispatch, #addRoute, #path, _basePath, routes, #clone, #notFoundHandler.
- Simple note: Implements the main application lifecycle from request entry via fetch/request to handler execution through composed middleware chain.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports the PatternRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter which compiles routes to RegExp patterns for matching, handling named parameters with optional regex patterns.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, ROUTES, Route, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, add, match.
- Simple note: Converts paths to Regexps on add(), then executes match() against all stored patterns for each request.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Re-exports the LinearRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter which performs linear iteration through stored routes matching by exact path comparison and label extraction.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, ROUTES, ROUTES_LOOP, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, checkOptionalParameter, hasStar, hasLabel.
- Simple note: Iterates all routes linearly on match(), handling wildcards and labels without precompilation to regex.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, buildAndExportAllMatchers.
- Simple note: Provides both runtime building and prepared serialization for serverless deployment.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter which builds a static matcher from routes using Trie-based path compilation and regular expression matching.

- Simple note: Routes are stored during add(), compiled to regex on first match() call, then cached for fast subsequent matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Names noticed: Matcher, MatcherMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, emptyParam, match, ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, ParamStash.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, RelocateMap, buildAndExportAllMatchers.
- Simple note: Allows routes to be compiled once and the regex + relocate map to be serialized as a string for minimal runtime bundles.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for trie-based path structure, handling insertion of routes and building RegExp patterns from the tree.
- Names noticed: Node, PATH_ERROR, ParamAssocArray, Context, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, compareKey, insert, buildRegExpStr.
- Simple note: Trie nodes store children, methods, and varIndex for pattern parameters; buildRegExpStr() generates regex from trie structure.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie data structure that manages Nodes and generates the final RegExp with index and param replacement maps.
- Names noticed: Trie, ReplacementMap, Context, ParamAssocArray, Node, insert, buildRegExp, clearWildcardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp.
- Simple note: Processes tokens from paths, inserts into trie, then builds RegExp with sparse indexReplacementMap and paramReplacementMap.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Names noticed: SmartRouter, activeRouter, routers.
- Simple note: Re-exports the SmartRouter class from router.ts.

- Names noticed: SmartRouter, activeRouter, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, routers, routes, #add, #match.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

## src/hono.ts

## src/hono-base.ts

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Names noticed: PatternRouter.

- Simple note: Converts path patterns like /:id or /:id{name} to RegExps during add(). match() loops routes array and execs RegExps.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Exports LinearRouter from sub module.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Index re-export file.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: LinearRouter implements linear iteration matching with optional param handling.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, RegExpMatchArrayWithIndices, ROUTES_LOOP.
- Simple note: Stores routes as [method, path, handler] array. match() loops sequentially. Handles wildcards (*), labels (:name), and optional parameters via checkOptionalParameter.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter from sub module.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
- Simple note: Index re-exports router class and prepared router for serialization.

usage.answer:
## Summary

**Bug Found:** In `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs`, the `addItem()` function mutated the original state object directly by assigning `const next = state`. This violated immutability and caused `state.items` to be modified.

**Fix Applied:** Created a shallow copy of the state with a new items array, then modified the copy:

| Line | Before | After |
|------|--------|-------|
| 6 | `const next = state` | `const next = { items: [...state.items], total: state.items.length }` |
| 7 | `next.items.push(item)` | `next.items.push(item)` (same - now safe on copy) |
| 8 | `next.total = state.items.length` | `next.total = next.items.length` (after push) |

**Verification:** Ran `node --test bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs` — test passes.

Identify concrete risks from the actual code, including missing amount validation and gateway error handling. Do not modify bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs.

usage.answer:
Now I can analyze the code and create the diagnosis document. Let me create the findings:

## Code Review Diagnosis

### ## Findings

### 1. Missing Amount Validation in `chargeCustomer` (Line 3)

**Risk Level:** Critical  
**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** The function accepts an `amount` parameter but performs no validation:
- No check for negative amounts
- No check for zero amounts
- No validation of amount type (number, string, etc.)
- No check for excessively large amounts that could indicate fraud

**Code:**
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // amount is used directly without validation
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:** An attacker could charge arbitrary negative amounts (resulting in refunds) or extremely large amounts to drain customer accounts.

---

### 2. Missing Amount Validation in `refundCustomer` (Line 8)

**Risk Level:** Critical  
**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** The `refundCustomer` function also accepts `amount` but has no validation whatsoever. Additionally, it lacks an ID check even though `chargeCustomer` validates customer ID.

**Code:**
```javascript
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)  // no validation at all
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:** Similar to charge, allows negative amounts (creating credits), zero amounts, and potentially excessive amounts.

---

### 3. Unhandled Gateway Errors (Lines 3, 8)

**Risk Level:** High  
**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** Both functions call gateway methods but never handle errors:
- No try-catch blocks
- No error logging or monitoring
- Gateway failures are silently ignored
- No error details returned to caller

**Code:**
```javascript
gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // errors swallowed
gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)  // errors swallowed
```

**Impact:**
- Payment failures are not reported to users
- Financial discrepancies go undetected (charges that fail but return `ok: true`)
- No audit trail of failed transactions
- Silent failures make debugging impossible

---

### 4. Missing Customer ID Validation in `refundCustomer`

**Risk Level:** Medium  
**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

**Issue:** While `chargeCustomer` validates `customer.id`, `refundCustomer` does not. This creates an inconsistency and potential for refunds without valid customers.

**Code:**
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  // ...
}

export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  // Missing: if (!customer.id) ...
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Impact:** Allows refunds to be processed for customers with missing or falsy IDs.

---

### ## Residual Risks

1. **Type Coercion Issues:** Even with amount validation, JavaScript's type coercion could allow bypasses (e.g., `"100"` or `100.000000001`). A more robust validation using strict type checks or schema validation is recommended.

2. **Insufficient Error Recovery:** While adding error handling addresses immediate issues, the system lacks a retry mechanism for transient gateway failures. Payment systems should implement exponential backoff and retry strategies.

3. **Audit Trail Gap:** There is no transaction logging. Even with improved error handling, financial systems require comprehensive audit trails for compliance and dispute resolution.

4. **Missing Idempotency:** Neither function appears to support idempotency keys. In distributed systems, retrying failed requests without idempotency can lead to duplicate charges.

5. **No Customer State Verification:** Functions assume the customer and gateway state are valid without verification, potentially allowing charges on invalid or frozen accounts.

---

### Verification Status

| Finding | Severity | Mitigation Status |
|---------|----------|-------------------|
| Missing amount validation | Critical | Requires code changes |
| Missing gateway error handling | High | Requires code changes |
| Missing customer ID validation (refund) | Medium | Requires code changes |
| Type coercion risks | Medium | Mitigated by adding strict validation |

**Note:** All identified issues require code modifications to the `payment.mjs` file as per task constraints. This review document serves as the artifact of the analysis.

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

File: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`

## Findings

### Missing Amount Validation
- **Location**: `chargeCustomer` (lines 1-5), `refundCustomer` (lines 7-10)
- **Risk**: Both functions accept `amount` parameter without any validation
- **Impact**: Negative amounts, zero amounts, or excessively large amounts could be processed without validation

### Missing Gateway Error Handling
- **Location**: Lines 3, 8
- **Risk**: Gateway calls (`gateway.charge`, `gateway.refund`) are invoked without error handling or return value inspection
- **Impact**: Payment failures, timeouts, or gateway exceptions will be silently ignored, returning `{ ok: true }` even when operations fail

### Missing Customer ID Validation in Refund
- **Location**: Line 8 (`refundCustomer`)
- **Risk**: `customer.id` check exists in `chargeCustomer` but is completely missing from `refundCustomer`
- **Impact**: Refunds can be attempted with `undefined` customer.id

### Inconsistent Return Values
- **Location**: Lines 4, 9
- **Risk**: Both functions return `{ ok: true }` without any details about the actual charge/refund result
- **Impact**: No visibility into transaction ID, failure codes, or gateway response details

## Residual Risks

1. **Silent Failures**: All gateway errors are swallowed, making debugging and customer support impossible
2. **Negative/Invalid Amounts**: Could lead to unexpected financial losses or accounting errors
3. **Incomplete Error Surface**: Missing `customer.id` check in refund creates a pathway for operations with undefined customers
4. **No Transaction Tracking**: Returned values provide no transaction identifiers for reconciliation

usage.answer:

**File analyzed**: `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs`
**Constraints satisfied**: Source file not modified

## Summary

The file contains two payment functions with critical issues:

| Finding | Severity | Impact |

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### 1. Missing Customer Amount Validation in `chargeCustomer`

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:2`

**Issue:** The `chargeCustomer` function does not validate the `amount` parameter before processing the charge.

**Current Code:**
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Risk:** Without validation, malicious users could pass:
- Negative amounts (money laundering attempt)
- Zero amounts (transaction flooding)
- Extremely large amounts (denial of service via payment provider)
- Non-numeric values (injection attacks)

**Impact:** HIGH - Potential financial loss, compliance violation, and payment processor abuse.

---

### 2. Missing Customer Error Handling in `refundCustomer`

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8`

**Issue:** The `refundCustomer` function does not validate the `customer` parameter.

**Current Code:**
```javascript
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)
  return { ok: true }
}
```

**Risk:** Calling `gateway.refund` without checking if `customer.id` exists can cause:
- Silent failures when `customer.id` is undefined
- Potential errors that crash the application
- Inconsistent behavior between charge and refund flows

**Impact:** MEDIUM - Silent failures lead to untracked financial discrepancies.

---

### 3. Missing Gateway Error Handling in Both Functions

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:3,8`

**Issue:** Both functions call gateway methods without checking for errors or handling failures.

**Current Code:**
```javascript
export function chargeCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  gateway.charge(customer.id, amount)  // No error handling
  return { ok: true }                 // Assumes success
}

export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  gateway.refund(customer.id, amount)  // No error handling
  return { ok: true }                  // Assumes success
}
```

**Risk:** Gateway operations can fail due to:
- Network timeouts or failures
- Payment provider API rate limits
- Insufficient funds
- Invalid card/transaction states
- Gateway service outages

**Impact:** HIGH - Silent failures cause incorrect state reporting (returns `ok: true` even on failure) and no retry mechanism.

---

### 4. No Transaction ID Capture or Logging

**File:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:1-10`

**Issue:** The functions do not capture or return transaction identifiers from gateway calls.

**Risk:** Without transaction IDs:
- Cannot audit payment/refund history
- Unable to handle disputes or chargebacks
- No traceability for financial reconciliation
- Cannot retry failed transactions

**Impact:** MEDIUM - Critical for financial compliance and customer support.

---

## Residual Risks

After addressing the above findings, the following residual risks remain:

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation Status |
|------|------------|-------------------|
| Business logic fraud (duplicate charges) | Medium | Depends on gateway deduplication |
| Race conditions in refund processing | Low | Requires concurrent access controls |
| Legacy gateway incompatibility | Low | Depends on gateway interface stability |

### Recommended Next Steps

1. **Add amount validation** with min/max bounds and type checking
2. **Implement error propagation** from gateway calls
3. **Return transaction metadata** (ID, timestamp, gateway response)
4. **Add logging** for audit trail
5. **Add transaction idempotency** support

### Verification Status

- [x] Source file reviewed (no modifications made)
- [x] Concrete risks identified with line references
- [x] Impact assessment completed
- [x] Residual risks catalogued

artifacts.content:
# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### CRITICAL: Missing Amount Validation

**Location:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:3`, `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8`

**Issue:** Both `chargeCustomer()` and `refundCustomer()` functions accept `amount` parameter without any validation.

**Risks:**
- Negative amounts could create credit fraud opportunities
- Extremely large amounts could cause system resource exhaustion or financial loss
- Zero amounts may bypass payment intent triggers

**Impact:** HIGH - Direct financial exposure and system abuse vectors

**Recommendation:**
```javascript
// Add amount validation before calling gateway
function validateAmount(amount) {
  if (typeof amount !== 'number' || amount <= 0 || amount > MAX_AMOUNT) {
    throw new Error('Invalid amount');
  }
  return true;
}
```

### CRITICAL: Missing Gateway Error Handling

**Location:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:3-4`, `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8-9`

**Issue:** Gateway methods (`gateway.charge()` and `gateway.refund()`) are called without checking return values or errors.

**Risks:**
- Failed transactions will be reported as successful
- No retry logic for transient failures
- Silent failures in production will go undetected
- No visibility into gateway-side issues (network, timeouts, rate limits)

**Impact:** HIGH - Silent data corruption and false reconciliation

**Recommendation:**
```javascript
const result = gateway.charge(customer.id, amount);
if (!result.ok) {
  throw new Error(`Gateway error: ${result.error}`);
}
```

### MEDIUM: Missing Customer Validation in refundCustomer()

**Location:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:8`

**Issue:** `refundCustomer()` lacks the `customer.id` check present in `chargeCustomer()`, allowing calls with undefined customer ID.

**Risks:**
- Gateway calls with undefined/null customer ID may fail unpredictably
- Inconsistent validation between charge and refund operations
- Potential security issue if customer context is compromised

**Impact:** MEDIUM - Inconsistency and potential silent failures

**Recommendation:**
```javascript
export function refundCustomer(customer, amount, gateway) {
  if (!customer.id) return { ok: false, error: 'missing customer' }
  // ... rest of implementation with error handling
}
```

### MEDIUM: Missing Transaction Details in Response

**Location:** `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:4`, `bench-targets/review/src/payment.mjs:9`

**Issue:** Success responses return only `{ ok: true }` with no transaction metadata.

**Risks:**
- No transaction ID for reconciliation
- No timestamp for audit trails
- No gateway reference for dispute resolution

**Impact:** MEDIUM - Audit and compliance issues

**Recommendation:**
```javascript
return { ok: true, transactionId: result.transactionId };
```

### LOW: Missing Type Safety

**Location:** Entire file

**Issue:** No TypeScript types or JSDoc annotations for function parameters and return values.

**Risks:**
- Runtime type errors possible with incorrect argument types
- No IDE autocompletion or type checking
- Difficult to verify contract adherence

**Impact:** LOW - Development ergonomics and runtime safety

**Recommendation:** Add TypeScript types or JSDoc annotations.

---

## Residual Risks

| Risk | Mitigation Status | Notes |
|------|------------------|-------|
| Financial loss from invalid amounts | Requires code change | Amount validation needed before merge |
| Silent transaction failures | Requires code change | Gateway error handling critical |
| Audit trail gaps | Requires code change | Transaction metadata required |
| Type-related runtime errors | Mitigated by testing | Can be caught in test suite |

**Recommended Actions (Must Have):**
1. Add amount validation with min/max bounds
2. Implement gateway error propagation
3. Add customer ID validation to refundCustomer()

**Recommended Actions (Should Have):**
4. Include transaction metadata in responses
5. Add TypeScript types or JSDoc annotations

**Reviewer:** Automated code review system
**Date:** 2026-07-21
**Severity Distribution:** CRITICAL (2), MEDIUM (2), LOW (1)

# Code Review Diagnosis

## Findings

### R1: Missing amount validation

Charge/refund without amount bounds. `amount` can be negative, NaN, or overflow. Attackers drain accounts.

**Fix:** Validate `amount > 0` before calling gateway.

### R2: No gateway error handling

`gateway.charge` and `gateway.refund` can throw or return failure. Current code ignores errors and returns `{ ok: true }`.

**Fix:** Wrap in try/catch and inspect return value. Reject on error.

### R3: Missing customer validation

`chargeCustomer` checks `customer.id` but `refundCustomer` does not.

**Fix:** Add `if (!customer.id)` check in `refundCustomer`.

### R4: Silent failure path

No logging or diagnostics when payment fails. Operations team blind to issues.

**Fix:** Add structured error messages with context (operation, customer, amount).

## Residual Risks

- Gateway integration may have side effects (lock accounts, debit pending).
- Amount validation only covers numerical bounds; currency normalization not enforced.
- Customer object tampering possible if caller bypasses auth.

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

This module provides a simple in-memory cache implementation using `createCache()`.

## createCache()

Creates a new cache instance with methods for storing and retrieving key-value pairs.

```javascript
import { createCache } from './cache'

const cache = createCache()
```

## Methods

The cache object returned by `createCache()` has the following methods:

### get(key)

Retrieves a value from the cache by key. Returns `undefined` if the key does not exist.

```javascript
cache.get(key)
```

### set(key, value)

Stores a value in the cache. Returns the set value.

```javascript
cache.set(key, value)
```

### delete(key)

Removes a key-value pair from the cache. Returns the deleted value or `true` if the key existed, `false` otherwise.

```javascript
cache.delete(key)
```

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

```javascript
cache.clear()
```

artifacts.content:
# Cache API

A simple in-memory cache API created from `bench-targets/docs/src/cache.mjs`.

## createCache()

Creates a new cache instance with an in-memory storage backed by a `Map`.

**Signature:**

```js
function createCache()
```

**Returns:** A cache object with the following methods: `get`, `set`, `delete`, and `clear`.

## Methods

### get(key)

Retrieves a value stored in the cache by key.

**Signature:**

```js
cache.get(key)
```

**Returns:** The value associated with the key, or `undefined` if the key does not exist.

---

### set(key, value)

Stores a key-value pair in the cache.

**Signature:**

```js
cache.set(key, value)
```

**Returns:** The value that was set.

---

### delete(key)

Deletes a key from the cache.

**Signature:**

```js
cache.delete(key)
```

**Returns:** `true` if the key existed and was deleted, `false` otherwise.

---

### clear()

Removes all entries from the cache.

**Signature:**

```js
cache.clear()
```

**Returns:** `undefined`.

---

## Example Usage

```js
import { createCache } from './bench-targets/docs/src/cache.mjs'

const cache = createCache()

// Set a value
cache.set('user:1', { name: 'Alice' })

// Get a value
const user = cache.get('user:1') // { name: 'Alice' }

// Delete a value
const exists = cache.delete('user:1') // true

// Clear the cache
cache.clear()
```

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase, sets up routing with a default SmartRouter combining RegExpRouter and TrieRouter, and exports the Hono class with generic type parameters for environment and schema.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, Router
- Simple note: Default constructor creates SmartRouter with [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] fallback chain.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base module containing Hono class implementation with routing core, HTTP method handlers (get, post, put, delete, etc.), middleware support via use(), and request dispatch through router.match().
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, Router, Context, Context, compose, getPath, mergePath, RouterRoute, METHOD_NAME_ALL, METHOD_NAME_ALL_LOWERCASE, FetchEventLike, MiddlewareHandler, NotFoundHandler, errorHandler
- Simple note: Implements chainable API for route registration and uses #dispatch() to call router.match() and compose middleware.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel export file for PatternRouter that re-exports the PatternRouter class from router.ts.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: Single export statement forwarding to router implementation.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PatternRouter using compiled RegExp patterns, supports wildcard matching with /*, optional parameters with ?, and transforms path segments into regex for fast matching.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, Route
- Simple note: Converts paths to regex with /:name and /* patterns, stores compiled patterns in #routes array.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel export file for LinearRouter that re-exports LinearRouter class from router.ts.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: Single export statement forwarding to router implementation.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements LinearRouter with linear iteration through stored routes, supports wildcard *, label patterns :name with optional regex constraints, and exact path matching without compilation overhead.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, ROUTES_LOOP, RegExpMatchArrayWithIndices, emptyParams, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe
- Simple note: Uses linear route iteration with explicit label and wildcard handling, no regex compilation during add().

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel export file for RegExpRouter family that exports RegExpRouter class and PreparedRegExpRouter with buildInitParams/serializeInitParams for serialization.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Exports core router and optimized serialized version for deployment.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements RegExpRouter using Trie structure to build combined regex patterns, stores routes in middleware and routes dictionaries, and builds matchers lazily on first match.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, ParamIndexMap, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, PATH_ERROR, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, nullMatcher, wildcardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp, clearWildcardRegExpCache, buildAllMatchers, #buildMatcher, add, match
- Simple note: Builds compiled regex from Trie on first match, caches middleware and routes in internal maps.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Defines Matcher interface and match function for RegExpRouter's return type [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap], includes emptyParam for no-match case.
- Names noticed: Matcher, MatcherMap, HandlerData, StaticMap, ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, emptyParam, match, ParamStash
- Simple note: Provides match function that returns regex, handler data array, and static path map.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements PreparedRegExpRouter with pre-built matchers and relocateMap for optimized lookup, supports adding paths dynamically after initial build using indexes and param maps.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, MatcherMap, Matcher, StaticMap, Router, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, emptyParam, match, #addWildcard, #addPath
- Simple note: Takes pre-computed matchers and relocateMap in constructor, uses indexes for fast path addition.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for Trie structure with recursive insert building regex strings, tracks #index for route endpoints and #varIndex for parameter mapping, and sorts child keys for consistent output.
- Names noticed: Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, PATH_ERROR, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_EXP_STR, regExpMetaChars, compareKey, insert, buildRegExpStr
- Simple note: Trie node recursively builds regex alternation with literal, pattern, and wildcard keys.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements Trie class with #root Node and context tracking for parameter indices, handles path groups with named placeholders, and builds regex with replacement maps for handler and param indices.
- Names noticed: Trie, Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, ReplacementMap, PATH_ERROR, insert, buildRegExp, #root
- Simple note: Replaces named groups {pattern} with markers before Trie insertion, generates replacement maps for final regex.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel export file for SmartRouter that re-exports SmartRouter class from router.ts.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: Single export statement forwarding to router implementation.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements SmartRouter that sequentially tries multiple routers, caches the first successful router as activeRouter, and uses UnsupportedPathError to continue trying next router when one fails.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, #routers, #routes, activeRouter, buildAllMatchers, add, match
- Simple name note: Tries routers in order, adopts first working router and binds its match function.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Barrel export file for TrieRouter that re-exports TrieRouter class from router.ts.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: Single export statement forwarding to router implementation.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements TrieRouter using recursive Node tree with #children for path segments and #methods per node for multiple HTTP verbs, uses checkOptionalParameter for /* patterns.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, Node, checkOptionalParameter, insert, search, #node, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL
- Simple note: Direct Trie-based routing with per-node method mapping, matches via recursive node traversal.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements Node class for Trie-based router with #children for path segments, #methods mapping handlers per method, and #patterns for regex-based segments, uses queue-based search with handler accumulation.
- Names noticed: Node, Router, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren, insert, #pushHandlerSets, search, #params
- Simple note: Matches via breadth-first queue traversal, accumulates handler sets for multi-match support.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Contains runTest helper for shared test suite across routers, validates basic usage, reserved words, complex patterns, registration order, multi-match scenarios, duplicate param names, and various edge cases.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, Vitest, getSuiteHierarchy, runTest, skip, newRouter, Params, Router, ParamIndexMap, match, Handler, Basic Usage, Reserved Words, Complex, Registration Order, Multi match, Duplicate param name
- Simple note: Defines common test cases that all routers should pass, parameterized with router instantiation.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests PatternRouter implementation by extending common case tests, adds skip for UnsupportedPath errors, includes duplicate param name and trailing slash specific tests.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Pattern, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, skip
- Simple note: Pattern-specific tests skip complex multi-match cases, validates leading/trailing slash handling.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests LinearRouter implementation by extending common case tests, adds skip for UnsupportedPath errors in multi-match params, includes skip for trailing slash support, adds specific test for unsupported label+wildcard combo.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, skip, Multi match, Trailing slash, Skip part
- Simple note: Skips label+star combo which throws UnsupportedPathError, tests optional parameter support.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests RegExpRouter implementation by extending common case tests, adds skip for unsupported patterns, includes tests for return type, UnsupportedPathError for ambiguous patterns, and complex capturing group validation.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, skip, Return value type, Ambiguous, Static and dynamic, Capture Group, Complex capturing group
- Simple note: Validates ambiguous pattern detection, tests regex construction and parameter stash extraction.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests SmartRouter implementation by using common case tests with RegExpRouter + TrieRouter combination, verifies router selection and chaining behavior.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: SmartRouter delegates to first successful router, inherits all tests via runTest.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests TrieRouter implementation by extending common case tests with newRouter using TrieRouter, validates all standard router behaviors.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest
- Simple note: Full common test suite coverage for Trie-based routing.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests Node class from trie-router with root node behavior, basic usage, named parameters, wildcards, regex patterns, multi-match scenarios, sort order, duplicate param handling, and routing order.
- Names noticed: Node, Root Node, Basic Usage, Name path, Name path - Multiple route, Param prefix, Named params and a wildcard, Wildcard, Regexp, All, Special Wildcard, Default with wildcard, Multi match, Duplicate param name, Sort Order, star, Routing order With named parameters
- Simple note: Exhaustive Node-level tests covering all TrieRouter features and edge cases.

## Cross-File Checklist

- pattern-router: src/router/pattern-router/index.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.ts, src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts
- linear-router: src/router/linear-router/index.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.ts, src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts
- reg-exp-router: src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts, src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts
- smart-router: src/router/smart-router/index.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.ts, src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts
- trie-router: src/router/trie-router/index.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.ts, src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts, src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a mechanical read-through ledger of routing-related source files in the Hono repository, documenting structure, key identifiers, and observations for each file.

---

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main Hono class entry point that extends HonoBase and provides the default SmartRouter configuration with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter fallback.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema, constructor, super, SmartRouter constructor with routers array.
- Simple note: Default router is SmartRouter wrapping [RegExpRouter, TrieRouter] for maximum compatibility.

---

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Base Hono class providing core request handling, middleware composition, error handling, and routing delegation.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, HonoOptions, ErrorHandler, errorHandler, onError, compose, run, handler, Context, NotFoundHandler, notFoundHandler, #handleError, MESSAGE_NO_RESPONSE, c, req, res.
- Simple note: Central orchestrator that delegates matching to router and handles middleware execution flow.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module barrel file that exports PatternRouter from router.ts.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter.
- Simple note: Re-export entry point for PatternRouter.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: PatternRouter implementation using regex patterns for path matching, supporting dynamic segments with capture groups and optional trailing wildcards.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, emptyParams, Route type as [RegExp, string, T] tuple, #routes array, add method, match method, path matching with RegExp compilation, wildcard support, optional parameter endings (?), regex escaping.
- Simple note: Collects all routes into a regex array and matches against them linearly; supports `:param` and `/:param{pattern}` syntax.

---

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module barrel file that exports LinearRouter from router.ts.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter.
- Simple note: Re-export entry point for LinearRouter.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: LinearRouter implementation using string-based path matching with direct path comparison and label extraction.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, Router, Params, Result, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, emptyParams, ROUTES_LOOP label, splitPathRe pattern, splitByStarRe pattern, routePath, handler, param extraction, star segment handling.
- Simple note: Linear string comparison approach with no trie/regex build; handles `*` wildcards and `:label` segments with optional capture groups.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module barrel file that exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter components.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams.
- Simple note: Exports both eager build and lazy-prepended router implementations.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: RegExpRouter implementation that defers matcher building until first match, using trie for path structure and compiling to regex.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, Router, ParamIndexMap, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, METHOD_NAME_ALL, UnsupportedPathError, checkOptionalParameter, nullMatcher, wildcardRegExpCache, buildWildcardRegExp, clearWildcardRegExpCache, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, Trie, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap, emptyParam, match function, findMiddleware, #middleware, #routes, add method, match delegate, buildAllMatchers, #buildMatcher, HandlerWithMetadata type as [T, number].
- Simple note: Two-phase router: collects routes, builds trie-based regex on first match; caches compiled matcher; handles wildcard routes specially.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Matcher types and match function that executes against the compiled [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap].
- Names noticed: ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, METHOD_NAME_ALL, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher as [RegExp, HandlerData[], StaticMap], MatcherMap as Record, emptyParam, match function, emptyParam constant.
- Simple note: Wraps the core matching logic using the compiled regex matcher tuple and static map fallback.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: PreparedRegExpRouter for serverless environments that pre-computes matchers during initialization.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, Router, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, RelocateMap, #matchers, #relocateMap, #addWildcard, #addPath, add method, buildAllMatchers, path matching, matcher serialization, JSON.stringify with RegExp handling, handlerData as [T, ParamIndexMap].
- Simple note: Pre-computes all matchers and relocate map at construction; matchers are serialized as JSON strings with regex embedded.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Node class for trie-based path parsing with regex generation.
- Names noticed: Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, PATH_ERROR symbol, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR constant as '[^/]+', ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR constant as '.*', TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR constant as '(?:|/.*)', regExpMetaChars Set, compareKey function, #index, #varIndex, #children, insert method, buildRegExpStr method, token processing, pattern matching, wildcard handling.
- Simple note: Builds trie structure from path tokens; generates regex strings with capture groups for labeled segments; sorts children by specificity.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Trie class that transforms path strings into token arrays for Node insertion and builds final regex with parameter maps.
- Names noticed: Trie, Context, ParamAssocArray, Node, ReplacementMap, #context, #root, insert method, buildRegExp method, group parsing with {pattern} replacement, token matching, regexp generation, captureIndex tracking, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap.
- Simple note: Splits paths into tokens and groups capture patterns; generates final regex with `#handlerIndex` and `@paramIndex` markers for replacement.

---

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module barrel file that exports SmartRouter from router.ts.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter.
- Simple note: Re-export entry point for SmartRouter.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: SmartRouter implementation that tries multiple routers sequentially and caches the successful one.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, Router, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, #routers array, #routes array, #activeRouter property, constructor, add method, match method, router.fallback, activeRouter getter, router name update.
- Simple note: Wraps multiple routers in sequence; delegates matching to first successful router; caches active router after first successful match.

---

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module barrel file that exports TrieRouter from router.ts.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter.
- Simple note: Re-export entry point for TrieRouter.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: TrieRouter implementation using a trie structure for path matching without regex compilation.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Router, checkOptionalParameter, Node, #node, add method, match method, path splitting, handler insertion.
- Simple note: Direct trie search approach; no regex compilation; each node stores methods and handlers.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Node class implementing trie traversal with pattern matching and multiple method support.
- Names noticed: Node, Params, METHOD_NAME_ALL, Pattern, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, emptyParams, hasChildren, #methods, #children, #patterns, #order, #params, insert method, #pushHandlerSets, search method, part iteration, pattern matching with matcher types, wildcard handling, path component counting.
- Simple note: Queue-based DFS search through trie; supports labeled patterns and wildcards; collects multiple handlers; sorts by insertion order for deterministic ordering.

---

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Shared test infrastructure providing runTest helper for common routing test cases across all routers.
- Names noticed: RunnerTestSuite, ParamIndexMap, Params, Router, getSuiteHierarchy, runTest, task, skip, newRouter, beforeEach, describe, it, expect, skipTask, router instance, match function, handler return type.
- Simple note: Provides test framework for common test suites including static paths, dynamic segments, wildcards, trailing slashes, and method handling.

---

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: PatternRouter-specific tests covering duplicate param names and trailing slash behavior.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, PatternRouter, Duplicate param name self test, Trailing slash test, router.add and router.match calls, expect assertions.
- Simple note: Tests PatternRouter allowing trailing slash matches and rejecting duplicate param names in same path.

---

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: LinearRouter-specific tests covering multi-match errors, param-per-handler behavior, and trailing slash handling.
- Names noticed: UnsupportedPathError, runTest, LinearRouter, Multi match with params per handler test, Capture regex pattern with trailing wildcard test, Complex parameter with regexp test, Trailing slash test, Skip part test, router.add and router.match calls.
- Simple note: Tests LinearRouter allowing trailing slash matches and rejecting label+star combinations.

---

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: RegExpRouter-specific tests covering duplicate params, capture groups, unsupported paths, and static/dynamic path ordering.
- Names noticed: ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, RegExpRouter, Duplicate param name parent/child tests, Capture Group Complex capturing group test, Capture complex multiple directories test, Complex parameter with regexp test, Including slashes test, Static and dynamic ordering tests, long label test, Different regular expression test, Multiple handlers with different label test, router.add and router.match calls.
- Simple note: Tests RegExpRouter rejecting ambiguous routes, complex capture groups, and static/dynamic route ordering conflicts.

---

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: SmartRouter tests verifying it correctly delegates to RegExpRouter followed by TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: runTest, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, SmartRouter, routers array with two routers, newRouter factory, basic routing tests from common suite.
- Simple note: Verifies SmartRouter fallback behavior by testing with RegExpRouter first, then TrieRouter.

---

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: TrieRouter tests using the common test suite.
- Names noticed: runTest, TrieRouter, basic routing tests including static paths, dynamic segments, wildcards, trailing slashes.
- Simple note: Tests TrieRouter with full common test suite for standard routing scenarios.

---

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Trie router node tests verifying insert and search behavior.
- Names noticed: basic insert and search operations, node behavior, handler placement, path traversal.
- Simple note: Tests node-level trie operations including insertion order and search result ordering.

---

## Cross-File Checklist

### pattern-router
- src/router/pattern-router/index.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.ts
- src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

### linear-router
- src/router/linear-router/index.ts
- src/router/linear-router/router.ts

# Routing Read/Write Ledger

This document provides a mechanical record of all router-related source files in the Hono repository, documenting each file's purpose, key names, and a simple note about its function.

## src/hono.ts

- Purpose: Main entry point for the Hono framework; exports the Hono class that extends HonoBase.
- Names noticed: Hono, HonoBase, HonoOptions, RegExpRouter, SmartRouter, TrieRouter, BlankEnv, BlankSchema, Env, Schema
- Simple note: Default constructor creates SmartRouter with RegExpRouter and TrieRouter as fallback routers.

## src/hono-base.ts

- Purpose: Core base class containing request handling logic, handler composition, and router abstraction.
- Names noticed: HonoBase, Context, Router, METHODS, METHOD_NAME_ALL, compose, Context, FetchEventLike, H, mergePath, getPath, notFoundHandler, errorHandler
- Simple note: Implements the main fetch/request/dispatch cycle and stores routes in a routes array and router instance.

## src/router/pattern-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for PatternRouter.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports PatternRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements pattern-based routing using compiled regular expressions for path matching.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, Route, emptyParams, UnsupportedPathError, endsWithWildcard, match, groups
- Simple note: Converts paths to RegExp patterns and matches by iterating through all routes linearly.

## src/router/linear-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for LinearRouter.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports LinearRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/linear-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements linear sequential route matching with support for wildcards and labeled parameters.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, ROUTES_LOOP, ROUTES_*, ROUTES_*, splitPathRe, splitByStarRe, ROUTES_*, ROUTES_*, ROUTES_*, emptyParams, UnsupportedPathError, hasStar, hasLabel
- Simple note: Matches routes in insertion order; supports * wildcards and :label parameters with special cases for combinations.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for RegExpRouter.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, PreparedRegExpRouter, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams
- Simple note: Exports RegExpRouter and PreparedRegExpRouter for production use cases.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a high-performance router using compiled regular expressions built from a trie.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, match, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, UnsupportedPathError, PATH_ERROR, buildWildcardRegExp, buildMatcherFromPreprocessedRoutes, findMiddleware, nullMatcher, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, emptyParam, clearWildcardRegExpCache, wildcardRegExpCache
- Simple note: Routes are processed once during add() then compiled to a single RegExp for fast matching.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/matcher.ts

- Purpose: Provides the match function and type definitions for the router's matcher interface.
- Names noticed: match, emptyParam, ParamIndexMap, Result, Router, HandlerData, StaticMap, Matcher, MatcherMap
- Simple note: The match function builds all matchers on first call and binds the router's match method.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/prepared-router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a pre-compiled router instance for deployment scenarios requiring serialized routing state.
- Names noticed: PreparedRegExpRouter, RelocateMap, buildInitParams, serializeInitParams, addWildcard, addPath
- Simple note: Accepts pre-built matchers and a relocateMap for serializing routing configuration.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements a trie node structure for building routing regular expressions.
- Names noticed: PATH_ERROR, Node, Context, ParamAssocArray, compareKey, regExpMetaChars, LABEL_REG_EXP_STR, ONLY_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR, TAIL_WILDCARD_REG_EXP_STR
- Simple note: Nodes store children as patterns and build regex strings during trie traversal.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/trie.ts

- Purpose: Implements the trie data structure that orchestrates node insertion and regex generation.
- Names noticed: Trie, ReplacementMap, Node, ParamAssocArray, Context, ReplacementMap, indexReplacementMap, paramReplacementMap, captureIndex
- Simple note: Converts path strings into tokens, inserts into trie, and generates combined regex with replacement maps.

## src/router/smart-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for SmartRouter.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports SmartRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/smart-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements a router orchestrator that tries multiple routers and commits to the first successful one.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, UnsupportedPathError, MESSAGE_MATCHER_IS_ALREADY_BUILT, activeRouter, activeRouter
- Simple note: Iterates through configured routers and caches the match function from whichever router succeeds.

## src/router/trie-router/index.ts

- Purpose: Module export file for TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter
- Simple note: Re-exports TrieRouter class from router.ts.

## src/router/trie-router/router.ts

- Purpose: Implements trie-based routing for efficient path matching.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, Node, checkOptionalParameter
- Simple note: Delegates to Node for insert and search operations; simple single-trie structure.

## src/router/trie-router/node.ts

- Purpose: Implements the trie node structure with method handlers and pattern matching.
- Names hasChildren, emptyParams, Pattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath, HandlerSet, HandlerParamsSet, HandlerParamsSet, possibleKeys, score, getPattern, splitPath, splitRoutingPath
- Simple note: Nodes contain children, method handlers, and patterns; uses multi-path search with queue for wildcard matching.

## src/router/common.case.test.ts

- Purpose: Provides a shared test runner function for common router test suites.
- Names noticed: runTest, RunnerTestSuite, skip, newRouter, match, Common, Basic, Reserved, Complex, Registration, Multi, Duplicate, page, star, Optional, All, long, Including, Capture
- Simple note: Abstracts beforeEach setup and test suite organization for router tests.

## src/router/pattern-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for PatternRouter with skipped cases for unsupported features.
- Names noticed: PatternRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, Duplicate, Trailing, skip, reason
- Simple note: Uses runTest helper; skips tests requiring features PatternRouter does not support.

## src/router/linear-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for LinearRouter with skipped cases for unsupported features.
- Names noticed: LinearRouter, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, Multi, params, Skip, Trailing, skip, reason
- Simple note: Uses runTest helper; skips complex wildcard tests not supported by LinearRouter.

## src/router/reg-exp-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for RegExpRouter including unsupported path error cases.
- Names noticed: RegExpRouter, ParamStash, UnsupportedPathError, runTest, Ambiguous, Multiple, parent, child, static, dynamic, different, Capture, Complex
- Simple note: Tests regex compilation and error handling for ambiguous path configurations.

## src/router/smart-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for SmartRouter using the RegExpRouter and TrieRouter combination.
- Names noticed: SmartRouter, RegExpRouter, TrieRouter, runTest, newRouter
- Simple note: Tests SmartRouter as a wrapper around other routers.

## src/router/trie-router/router.test.ts

- Purpose: Tests for TrieRouter using the common test suite.
- Names noticed: TrieRouter, runTest, newRouter
- Simple note: Full test coverage using runTest helper with no skips.

## src/router/trie-router/node.test.ts

- Purpose: Unit tests for the Node trie structure in TrieRouter.
- Names noticed: Node, Root, search, insert, get, post, put, name, params, wildcard, regexp, All, Special, Default, Multi, Sort, Duplicate, prefix, Order, initial
- Simple note: Comprehensive tests for trie node operations and path matching behavior.

## Cross-File Checklist

### smart-router

### trie-router
